title: Lastrea catoptera
taxonomy: Lastrea catoptera belongs to the family Athyriaceae and is a species of fern. It is classified within the genus Lastrea, which is a part of the order Polypodiales. The classification is further supported by its classification as a member of the class Polypodiopsida and division Pteridophyta. The species is native to tropical and subtropical regions, adding to its unique characteristics within the plant kingdom. Further research is needed to confirm its full classification.
uses: Lastrea catoptera, also known as the catoptera lady fern, has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. Its medicinal uses include treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems due to its potential anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also believed to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating infections. Furthermore, it may be used to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
